title: How a Team of Marines Built the Corps' FPV Drone Training Program from a Cold Start
gist: A podcast-episode blurb describing how a small team at Marine Corps Weapons Training Battalion, Quantico, took the Corps from having virtually no first-person-view (FPV) attack drones last fall to a Marine Corps-wide FPV drone training system.

insights.claim: The U.S. Marine Corps had virtually no first-person view (FPV) attack drones as of fall 2025, but has since stood up a Marine Corps-wide FPV drone training system, built from a cold start by a small team at Weapons Training Battalion, Quantico.
insights.speaker: Scott Cuomo, Steve Pearsoll, Troy Hotaling, Jude Stewart, Justin Creasman, Timothy Brockup, and Ryan Evans
insights.so_what: Signals a fast institutional pivot toward FPV attack-drone doctrine inside the Marine Corps, relevant for vendors and integrators bidding into emerging DoD small-drone training and procurement programs.
insights.quote: Last fall, the U.S. Marine Corps had virtually no first-person view attack drones.
insights.locator: Last fall, the U.S. Marine Corps had virtually no first-person view attack drones

insights.claim: The Marine Corps' FPV drone training program was built by a small internal team of one officer, two warrant officers, and three senior NCOs at Weapons Training Battalion, Quantico, led by Col. Scott Cuomo — illustrating a bottom-up, small-team model for standing up a new warfighting capability quickly rather than a large formal acquisition program.
insights.speaker: Scott Cuomo, Steve Pearsoll, Troy Hotaling, Jude Stewart, Justin Creasman, Timothy Brockup, and Ryan Evans
insights.so_what: A template for how the U.S. military can rapidly scale niche drone-warfare capability via small dedicated teams — worth watching as a replicable model across other services/units.
insights.locator: Ryan was pleased to welcome some great marines onto the show

source_meta.author: Scott Cuomo, Steve Pearsoll, Troy Hotaling, Jude Stewart, Justin Creasman, Timothy Brockup, and Ryan Evans
